page: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram is a Cloud GPU family server configured with an x86_64 architecture. It features 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Core Processor based on the Broadwell architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core.

This instance integrates four NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs, delivering a total of 64 GB of VRAM with 16 GB allocated per GPU. Local storage consists of a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, offering high-speed read and write capabilities. Network and connectivity features include one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark testing, the server achieved a stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 1766.38 and a multi-core CPU performance score of 41473.88, placing both metrics in the average category. The shared CPU allocation presents a performance tradeoff for compute-intensive workloads requiring dedicated CPU cycles, but the dense GPU-to-memory ratio provides high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that rely primarily on parallel GPU processing rather than raw single-core CPU performance.

This server configuration is suited for virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI), video streaming, transcoding, and moderate machine learning inference tasks that benefit from multi-GPU setups with high VRAM capacity and substantial system memory.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram is a Cloud GPU instance designed for parallel processing workloads. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 24 Intel Broadwell physical cores running at 2.0 GHz with shared CPU allocation. The server is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory and a 1200 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage. Its primary acceleration capability comes from four NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs, providing a total of 64 GB of VRAM. In performance testing, the instance achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 1766.38 and a multi-core score of 41473.88. Given its shared CPU allocation, it offers a cost-efficient profile for GPU-heavy tasks where CPU dedication is not critical. This configuration is highly suitable for virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI), video transcoding, and multi-GPU machine learning inference.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 24 Intel cores, 256 GB of system memory, a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, and four NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs providing a total of 64 GB of VRAM.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram is a Cloud GPU server with 24 Intel cores, 256 GB RAM, a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, and four NVIDIA A16 GPUs with 64 GB VRAM.
tagline: A high-density GPU server featuring four accelerators and substantial system memory for parallel processing and media workloads.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs providing 64 GB of total VRAM
24 Intel physical cores with 256.0 GB of system memory
1200 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Optimized for virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI) and video transcoding
